deep_dive.title: Differential Analysis of Multispectral Images for Terrain Identification
deep_dive.abstract: Reliable terrain understanding is a prerequisite for autonomous robot navigation. Yet, the widespread RGB-based perception can fail under low illumination, shadows, and material ambiguities. In this work we propose DRIFT, a lightweight multispectral framework that combines raw spectral bands and illumination-tolerant band-ratio representations through a dual-stream residual architecture and a differential fusion branch. Band ratios attenuate multiplicative acquisition effects (illumination/sensor gains), while the differential fusion explicitly highlights discrepancies between absolute-band and ratio-derived cues, which improves the robustness to noisy or partially unreliable spectral measurements. In the paper (i) we evaluate DRIFT on a new oil-on-soil multispectral dataset acquired using a MicaSense RedEdge-P camera mounted on an Unmanned Aerial Vehicle, and (ii) we provide an additional controlled study on water-on-grass under varying illumination and thermal perturbations (hot/cold water) to analyze NIR-sensitive effects. DRIFT consistently improves over strong baselines, while remaining compatible with edge deployment.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multispectral dual-stream fusion with differential branch for terrain identification

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes DRIFT, a multispectral framework combining raw bands and band-ratios via dual-stream residual architecture and differential fusion for robust terrain identification.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对传统RGB感知在低照明、阴影和材质歧义下失效的问题，提出DRIFT多光谱框架。该方法采用双流残差架构分别处理原始光谱波段和照度不变的波段比值，并通过差分融合分支凸显两者差异，提升对噪声和部分不可靠测量的鲁棒性。在油污地形数据集上验证了其优越性，为自主导航提供了可靠的地形理解方案。

quick_skim.title: Scalable and Trustworthy Earth Observation Foundation Models
quick_skim.abstract: Foundation models (FMs) have transformed machine learning from isolated task-specific model development toward general-purpose models pretrained on broad data and adapted to multiple downstream tasks. Earth observation (EO) is an important domain for this paradigm because satellite and airborne archives are large, high-revisit, and increasingly multimodal, while reliable field labels are often sparse. Remote sensing foundation models (RSFMs) cannot be transferred reliably/optimally without domain-specific adaptation. This is because EO data are governed by measurement physics and operational decision constraints. This chapter reviews the design principles arising from these domain-specific constraints. It first defines the FMs paradigm in remote sensing (RS), then synthesizes the current model landscape, pretraining objectives, architecture designs, downstream adaptation and trustworthiness requirements. The chapter also incorporates recent benchmark evidence showing that no single geospatial foundation model is universally best and that inconsistent evaluation remains a major issue to fair comparison and reliable deployment. In addition, two brief environmental monitoring case studies; physics-informed spectral targeted masking for harmful algal bloom prediction and reinforcement learning for adaptive environmental monitoring station selection to illustrate the FMs domain-guided principles in practice. This chapter posits that next-generation RSFMs should be evaluated not only by benchmark accuracy, but also by modality-aware transfer and physically plausible representations for trustworthy EO decisions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Reviews design principles for multimodal remote sensing foundation models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 综述多模态遥感基础模型的设计原则

quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 综述多模态遥感基础模型的设计原则
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Scalable and Trustworthy Earth Observation Foundation Models reviews design principles for building foundation models in remote sensing, emphasizing multimodal data and domain-specific adaptation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文系统回顾了遥感基础模型（RSFMs）的设计原则，指出由于地球观测数据的物理和操作约束，现有模型需要领域自适应。文章定义了RSFM范式，并讨论了多模态数据、尺度化、可解释性和可信度等关键问题，为构建多模态遥感基础模型提供了全面指导。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 多模态遥感图像分类与缺失模态学习相关文献检索, especially work that connects or combines: m; i; studies addressing learning methods with missing modalities in remote sensing data; methods for fusing multimodal remote sensing data from different sensors; find academic papers on multimodal remote sensing image classification and missing modality learning; retrieve studies about multimodal remote sensing data fusion and incomplete modality processing; explore deep learning approaches for handling missing modalities in multimodal remote sensing images.

quick_skim.title: LOGOS: Language-guided Oriented Object Detection in Aerial Scenes
quick_skim.abstract: Object detection in geospatial scenes, such as satellite and aerial imagery, poses significant challenges due to the varying orientations and densities of objects, as well as the complex backgrounds inherent to remote sensing imagery. Traditional methods for oriented object detection have struggled to address issues such as angular discontinuity, fixed query sizes, and inefficiencies in handling sparse or cluttered scenes. In this paper, we propose LOGOS, a novel transformer-based approach that leverages textual prompts to guide the detection of oriented objects in aerial scenes. In particular, our proposed approach incorporates prompt-modulated content queries to dynamically adjust the model's focus based on the provided text, thereby improving object detection accuracy in complex environments. Empirically, extensive experiments on the DOTA dataset demonstrate that LOGOS outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods, particularly in densely packed and rotated object scenarios. Our approach offers a significant step forward in improving the robustness and scalability of oriented object detection in remote sensing applications.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses textual prompts to guide oriented object detection in aerial imagery
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 在航空图像中使用文本提示引导有向目标检测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 在航空图像中使用文本提示引导有向目标检测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 在航空图像中使用文本提示引导有向目标检测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: LOGOS integrates textual prompts via prompt-modulated content queries in a transformer, enabling dynamic focus adjustment for oriented object detection in aerial scenes.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 遥感图像有向目标检测面临朝向变化、背景复杂等挑战。本文提出LOGOS，一种基于变换器的方法，通过文本提示调制内容查询，动态调整模型关注焦点。该方法融合语言和视觉模态，在密集和杂乱场景中提升了检测性能。实验表明，语言引导能有效辅助遥感目标检测，尤其对稀疏场景更有优势。该研究为遥感中的多模态学习提供了新思路。
